commit.message:
ui: remove pts labels from leaderboard and rescale score text

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add gtag config call — GA was deferring all hits

Moved gtag('js') + gtag('config') to queue in dataLayer BEFORE the
gtag.js script is appended. Previously they were called after
appendChild, creating a race where gtag.js could process the queue
before the config command was queued. Without the config command GA
defers all hits indefinitely (Tag Assistant: "config command not
provided").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Require wallet connection before swap in production

Prevents confusing error when attempting unsigned swap on mainnet.

feat: teacher performance grading + library for teacher & student portals

- center-svc: TeacherRepository.findByUserId + findByUserIdActive query

- GradeController: added GET /api/v1/grades/student/{id} (routable via api-gateway)
- api-gateway: added /api/v1/grades/** route → assess-svc

file count + total size display, upload progress indicator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: cotizacion no encontrada en cierre — 3 mejoras de robustez

1. _normNombre(): helper para comparacion insensible a acentos y
   espacios dobles (NFD + strip combining marks + collapsar espacios)

2. _cotizParaCierre(): usa _normNombre en la comparacion de nombres;
   retorna {emitida} cuando solo hay cotizaciones EMITIDA para ese
   cliente (permite diferenciar 'ya emitida' vs 'sin cotizacion')

3. abrirCierreDesdeCliente Caso 4: muestra mensaje especifico si la
   cotizacion existe pero ya fue marcada como EMITIDA (evita confusion
   con 'sin cotizacion registrada')

4. _syncFromSP race condition: captura dirty IDs como Set ANTES del
   await Promise.all — previene que _flushCotizaciones borre ._dirty
   durante la espera, lo que causaba que registros pendientes se
   sobreescribieran con datos de SP al completar el sync

Fix RFQ CAPTCHA verification: remove remoteip from Turnstile check

The remoteip parameter sent to Cloudflare's siteverify endpoint caused
verification failures when the client IP seen by API Gateway differed
from the IP Cloudflare recorded (e.g. behind proxy/VPN). Since remoteip
is optional, removing it fixes the mismatch. Also added error logging
for the Cloudflare response to aid future debugging.

commit.message:
Fix false victory log messages from MCTS simulation

MCTS simulation copies were triggering real log.info() and registry
operations in GameOutcomeService, producing misleading "wins!" messages.
Add a simulation flag to GameData and early-return in GameOutcomeService
to skip side effects. Other services already use NoOpSessionManager via
GameSimulator's isolated service graph, so no guards needed elsewhere.

commit.message:
fix: Resolve 4 remaining verified bugs (#8, #11, #12, #14)

#8 - SMTP connection leak (notifications.py):
Replace manual connect()/quit() with `async with aiosmtplib.SMTP(...) as smtp`,
which guarantees the connection is closed even when login() or send_message()
raises an exception.

#11 - S3 backup failure causes silent run accumulation (cleanup.py):
Both retention-runs and retention-days cleanup loops now count skipped runs
and emit a logger.warning when any runs are kept due to S3 backup failure,
making the accumulation visible in logs/monitoring without changing behavior.

#12 - TOCTOU between exists() and stat() in log cleanup (cleanup.py):
Replace the exists() guard + stat() pair with a single stat() call wrapped
in try/except FileNotFoundError. Eliminates the race window and removes the
redundant existence check.

#14 - start_date > end_date accepted silently by scheduler (api/scheduler.py):
Add an explicit check after parsing both dates and raise HTTP 422 with a
clear message when start_date >= end_date, preventing APScheduler from
silently scheduling a job that will never fire.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix subcategory dropdown resetting when categories query updates

The categories dependency in the main useEffect caused
selectedParentCatId to reset to 'none' every time React Query
refetched categories data, making the subcategory dropdown
disappear after selection. Split into two effects: main form
reset (no categories dep) and a separate sync effect for
reverse-lookup when editing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: Revise BUG_REPORT after manual code verification

After reading the actual code for each entry, 9 of the original 18 items
turned out to be false positives:

False positives removed:
- #2  Git-Sync lock: all ops confirmed inside the lock block
- #4  Scheduler multi-instance: single-instance architecture by design
- #6  JWT token type: get_session_by_token() prevents misuse
- #7  Log download rate limit: global 200/min default applies
- #9  Fire-and-forget notifications: intentional, errors are logged
- #10 Session generator leak: session.close() called in all finally blocks
- #13 Dependency audit cache race: APScheduler max_instances=1 prevents it
- #15 X-Forwarded-For bypass: config risk, not a code bug

Real remaining issues: #8 (SMTP leak), #11 (S3 orphaned runs),
#12 (log TOCTOU, trivial), #14 (silent scheduler date validation failure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix white screen: hooks called after early return in ProtectedRoute

React error #310 (too many re-renders) was caused by
useMobileAuthSheet() and useIsMobile() being called AFTER an
early return in ProtectedRoute. This violates React's rules of
hooks — hooks must always be called in the same order on every
render. Moved both hooks above the early return.

FormLabel/FormControl replaced with plain HTML to avoid
useFormField crash outside FormField context).

commit.message:
docs: Correct BUG_REPORT - mark #5 (SSH key TOCTOU) as non-issue

Python 3's tempfile.mkstemp() internally calls os.open() with mode 0o600,
so the file has restrictive permissions from the moment of creation.
The subsequent chmod(0o600) is redundant but harmless. No TOCTOU window exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Protect pipeline cache with threading.Lock to prevent race conditions

The global _pipeline_cache and _cache_timestamp variables were read and
written without synchronisation. APScheduler runs jobs in a thread pool,
so scheduled pipeline runs calling get_pipeline() / discover_pipelines()
could race against a concurrent git-sync that calls invalidate_cache().

Adds _cache_lock (threading.Lock) and wraps every read and write of the
two globals in a with _cache_lock block:
- discover_pipelines(): cache-hit check and cache update are each locked
  independently so the expensive directory scan runs without holding the
  lock (avoids blocking all callers for the duration of the scan)
- invalidate_cache(): both assignments are atomic under the lock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix build: add missing @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner to root deps

but @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner were only
listed in function-level package.json files. Added them to root
devDependencies and cast getSignedUrl args to work around @smithy/types
version conflicts with CDK packages.

commit.message:
fix: Improve container cleanup logging, CRON validation, and downstream trigger safety

- executor/core.py: Include container ID in cleanup warning so orphaned
  containers can be identified and removed manually (docker rm -f <id>)

- scheduler.py: Add _validate_cron_parts() helper that checks numeric
  CRON fields against valid ranges (e.g. hour 0-23, minute 0-59) before
  passing to APScheduler, preventing silent runtime failures

- dependency_audit.py: Apply the same CRON range validation when
  scheduling the dependency audit job; falls back to '0 3 * * *' with
  a descriptive warning instead of letting APScheduler raise later

- downstream_triggers.py: Check pipeline existence via get_pipeline()
  inside add() before enqueuing a downstream trigger; logs a clear
  warning and skips non-existent pipelines instead of producing vague
  errors downstream

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
